title: Dopamine Nation
author: Anna Lembke
subline: Pleasure and pain live on the same seesaw.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A seesaw: PLEASURE on the left (high) and PAIN on the right (also high) — both stacked. The book's thesis: the brain's baseline flattens between them. A red fulcrum. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1500)" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" text-anchor="middle">
    <!-- PLEASURE column: 5 stacked white boxes rising up -->
    <g transform="translate(-460 0)">
      <g stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="1" fill="none">
        <rect x="-120" y="0" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-100" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-200" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-300" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-400" width="240" height="80"/>
      </g>
      <text x="0" y="-440" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.18em">PLEASURE</text>
      <text x="0" y="120" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="0.18em">DRUGS · SEX · SCREENS</text>
    </g>
    <!-- PAIN column: 5 stacked red boxes rising up -->
    <g transform="translate(460 0)">
      <g stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1" fill="none">
        <rect x="-120" y="0" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-100" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-200" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-300" width="240" height="80"/>
        <rect x="-120" y="-400" width="240" height="80"/>
      </g>
      <text x="0" y="-440" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">PAIN</text>
      <text x="0" y="120" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">WITHDRAWAL · CRAVING</text>
    </g>
    <!-- A red horizontal fulcrum line in the middle, connecting the bases -->
    <line x1="-340" y1="80" x2="340" y2="80" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <text x="0" y="160" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">HOMEOSTASIS</text>
    <!-- A horizontal hairline through the middle indicating the baseline -->
    <line x1="-700" y1="0" x2="-220" y2="0" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 6"/>
    <line x1="220" y1="0" x2="700" y2="0" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 6"/>
    <!-- Tiny arrows showing the seesaw / brain adaptation -->
    <g stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1" fill="none">
      <line x1="-360" y1="-20" x2="-340" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="-360" y1="-20" x2="-380" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="360" y1="-20" x2="340" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="360" y1="-20" x2="380" y2="0"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: Two stacked columns: PLEASURE (white) on the left, PAIN (red) on the right. The book argues that high pleasure always brings matched pain, on a seesaw linked by homeostasis. Red is the only chromatic accent. 320px weight-500 title at the top.
